demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a live-in caregiver; bedbound and fully dependent on the caregiver for safety and medications

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call made by his live-in caregiver) He's been awake all night, but today he can barely open his eyes, his speech is slurred, and his hands are shaking violently. He has bad liver disease and I don't know what to do.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The caregiver says he has gone downhill fast over the last day or two — much more confused and drowsy than usual, and today she can barely wake him.

symptom_narrative.onset: getting worse over the past 2 days; the day-night sleep reversal has been building over the past week
symptom_narrative.duration: about 48 hours of worsening confusion and drowsiness, worst today
symptom_narrative.character: started as mild confusion, now he is deeply drowsy and only wakes if we shout at him; his speech is slurred and his hands shake and flap when he holds them out
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst he's ever been — today I can barely rouse him
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: he is asleep all day and awake all night; the drowsiness in the daytime has got much worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he's been badly constipated for about 4 days, and he stopped taking his sweet syrup medicine because he hates the taste
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is bedbound, can't do anything for himself, and I have to do everything for him

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing advanced liver scarring (cirrhosis) from years of heavy drinking
past_medical_history.how_managed: he stopped drinking a while ago and is now off alcohol; he takes a sweet syrup medicine for it

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a sweet, thick syrup laxative medicine for his liver (lactulose)

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: long history of heavy alcohol use, now abstinent
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: bedbound and completely dependent on a live-in caregiver for safety and for taking his medicines

objective_data_offered.value: he can barely open his eyes and only wakes up a little if we shout his name or shake his shoulder
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: caregiver's lay description of drowsiness, not a formal consciousness assessment

objective_data_offered.value: the whites of his eyes and his skin look yellow
when he holds his hands out they shake and flap, and his speech is slurred and mumbled
his belly looks swollen and bloated
a full, unopened bottle of his lactulose syrup is sitting on the nightstand — he has refused it for the last 4 days because of the taste
